title: PolymarketDecisionV0
description: Trading decision output contract for the polymarket-agents pipeline. Uses the same controlled-vocab enums as polymarket-agents committee/contracts.py RefereeOutput but is owned by this project. The fine-tuned model emits ONE JSON object matching this schema per inference. Pydantic validators enforce: probabilities sum to 1.0 ± 0.01; labels unique within object; controlled-vocab flags only.

summary.description: 1-3 sentence high-signal summary of the decision and the chief evidence.

global_confidence.description: Calibrated overall confidence the chosen side will resolve correctly. Reflects data quality + committee disagreement, NOT just argmax(probability).

choice_assessments.description: Per-outcome probability table. Binary markets: 2 entries (YES/NO). Multi-outcome markets: N entries (one per market outcome). Probabilities MUST sum to 1.0 ± 0.01.

label.description: Outcome label as it appears in the market metadata. e.g. 'YES', 'NO', 'Trump', 'Harris'.

confidence.description: Calibrated confidence in THIS specific probability estimate. Lower when evidence is thin or contradictory.

data_coverage.description: Fraction of relevant signal categories that had usable data.

contradictions.description: Explicit notes about contradicting evidence we considered.

key_points.description: Bullet-style key drivers for this assessment. 1-5 items typical.

uncertainty_flags.description: Controlled-vocab flags. Mirrors polymarket-agents UNCERTAINTY_FLAGS_ENUM exactly. Unknown values rejected.

trade_blockers.description: Controlled-vocab reasons the decision should NOT be executed even if probabilities favor a side.

rationale.description: Multi-paragraph reasoning trace linking evidence to probability. The fine-tuned model is being taught to emit this explicit step-by-step justification.

$comment: Pydantic-enforced invariants: sum(choice_assessments[].probability) in [0.99, 1.01]; choice_assessments[].label unique; argmax(probability) is implicit recommended_side.
